title: Taskmaster
content:
{{CharHub|Taskmaster|Antonia Dreykov}} {{Character |image = Taskmaster Infobox.jpg |real name = Antonia Dreykov (Антония Дрейкова)[https://marvelcinematicuniverse.fandom.com/f/p/4400000000003870095 Antonia Dreykov Thunderbolts* Bio] |alias = {{Alias |codenames = Taskmaster{{Ref|BW}}}} |species = [[Human]] ([[Cyborg]]) |citizenship = {{Citizenship|RUS}} |gender = Female |DOB = [[1990s|Late 1990s]]{{Ref|BW}} |DOD = [[2027|Fall 2027]]{{Ref|TB}} |affiliation = [[Red Room]] (formerly)
*[[Taskmaster Protocol]] (formerly)
{{Affiliation|OXE}} |movie = {{Infobox List|{{Collapse|''[[The Avengers]]'' (mentioned)
}}''[[Black Widow (film)|Black Widow]]''
''[[Thunderbolts*]]''}} |comic = {{Infobox List|{{Collapse|''[[Thunderbolts* Dossier]]'' (files)}}}} |actor = [[Olga Kurylenko]]
[[Ryan Kiera Armstrong]] (young)
[[Andy Lister]] (in-suit) |status = Deceased}} {{Quote|She watches everything and she can do it. She's a perfect mimic. And she fights just like all of your [[Avengers|friends]].|[[Dreykov]] to [[Natasha Romanoff]]|Black Widow (film)}} '''Antonia Dreykov''' (Russian: '''Антония Дрейкова''') was the daughter of General [[Dreykov]]. Having been gravely injured during an [[Budapest Operation|assassination attempt]] on her father, Antonia had her mind and body controlled by a chip, which imbued her with uncanny photographic reflexes that allow her to mimic the fighting techniques of other individuals, turning her into '''Taskmaster'''. Becoming the [[Red Room]]'s commander, Taskmaster had been ordered by her father to track down [[Yelena Belova]], who had taken their [[Red Dust]] and could free the other [[Black Widows]]. This put Taskmaster on a collision course with the person who nearly killed her years earlier, [[Natasha Romanoff]]. However, once Romanoff had killed Dreykov and destroyed the [[Red Room Academy]], Taskmaster became exposed to the Red Dust, freeing her mind from Dreykov's control and allowing her to then escape the Red Room. Taskmaster began working as an assassin for [[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]], but was ultimately betrayed by de Fontaine, who sent her on a mission to be killed by [[Ghost]]. ==Biography== {{MD|Antonia Dreykov (Earth-199999)}} ===Early Life=== ====Raised by General Dreykov==== Antonia Dreykov was born to General [[Dreykov]], leader of the [[Red Room]]. Unlike many other young girls, whom Dreykov would encounter throughout his life, Antonia was not forced to go through the [[Red Room Academy]] and be trained to become a skilled assassin and a member of the [[Black Widows]]. Instead, Antonia was actually allowed to attend a school while living in [[Budapest]], and maintained a close relationship with her father.{{Ref|BW}} ====[[Budapest Operation]]==== {{Quote|[[Dreykov]]'s daughter. You killed her."
"I had to. I needed her to lead me to Dreykov. Dreykov's daughter was collateral damage.|[[Yelena Belova]] and [[Natasha Romanoff]]|Black Widow (film)}} [[File:Young Antonia Dreykov (2).png|{{AltFloat}}|Dreykov visiting her [[Dreykov|father]] in his [[Dreykov's Budapest Office|office]]]] In [[2008]],{{Ref|BWDS}} following a day at school, Antonia Dreykov had gone to visit her [[Dreykov|father]] at his [[Dreykov's Budapest Office|office]] in [[Budapest]], where she happily ran up the stairs to greet her father, who was still working at that moment. However, not long after she had arrived, a bomb was detonated in the office, causing a massive fireball, caused by [[Black Widow]] and [[Hawkeye]], who were attempting to assassinate Dreykov. [[File:Budapest Operation.png|{{AltFloat}}|Dreykov is almost killed in an explosion]] Despite being right in the middle of the fireball, Antonia and her father barely survived the explosion, with Antonia's face becoming severely disfigured by the flames, much to her father's disgust.{{Ref|BW}} Believing her targets dead, Romanoff was haunted by the apparent death of Antonia for years.{{Ref|TA}} In order to save Antonia's life and stop her agonizing pain from the damage that she had sustained, Dreykov had inserted a chip into the back of her neck, nulling her senses and giving Dreykov complete control of her.{{Ref|BW}} ====Transformed into Taskmaster==== {{Quote|When your bomb [[Budapest Operation|exploded]], it nearly killed my Antonia. I had to put a chip in the back of her neck. In the back of her neck. Look at her. You find it difficult to look at her? I do.|[[Dreykov]] to [[Natasha Romanoff]]|Black Widow (film)}} Having survived the bombing that [[Natasha Romanoff]] and [[Clint Barton]] had arranged, Antonia had her emotions and senses disrupted by the chip that [[Dreykov]] had inserted into her head. With her under his complete control, Dreykov put his daughter through the [[Red Room]], where he discovered that she had a skill for mimicry, and could recreate her enemies' movements by simply studying them closely enough. Over the years, Antonia would successfully complete her training at the [[Red Room Academy]], becoming Dreykov's finest assassin. However, as Dreykov remained disgusted by the burns covering his daughter's face, he had provided her with her [[Taskmaster Suit|suit]], including a mask to hide her scars and identity, which also provided her with a HUD to assist with combat. Antonia was given the code name of Taskmaster, becoming the head of the [[Black Widows]], under Dreykov's command, who would only send her on the most important missions. To become the superior assassin, Taskmaster would watch footage of the [[Avengers]] in combat, studying their fighting styles, in order to mimic them in combat.{{Ref|BW}} ===Red Room Assassin=== ====Given Her Next Assignment==== [[File:Taskmaster Helmet.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster watching the [[Avengers]] fight]] {{Quote|Smile.|[[Lerato]] to Taskmaster|Black Widow (film)}} In [[2016|June 2016]], [[Yelena Belova]] had come across the [[Red Dust]] during a mission to assassinate [[Oksana]], and had subsequently escaped with it, General [[Dreykov]] leaded of this, and chose to recruit Taskmaster to get the Red Dust back.{{Ref|BW}} While Taskmaster had been preparing for her next mission by watching video footage of [[Black Widow]] fighting{{Ref|BWDS}} and the fight between [[Hawkeye]] and [[T'Challa|Black Panther]], she was interrupted by [[Lerato]], who installed new information into Taskmaster's helmet about the mission.{{Ref|BW}} ====[[Ambush on Natasha Romanoff]]==== [[File:BW FT 12.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster blocking [[Black Widow]]'s shots]] Taskmaster was deployed in [[Norway]] to intercept [[Natasha Romanoff]]'s transportation of the [[Red Dust]]. Taskmaster first attacked the spy by blasting her vehicle off the road with an explosive projectile and proceeded to approach her, but raised her [[Taskmaster's Shield|shield]] to deflect sudden gunfire, then hurled it at the spy and wedged it in her car window before the latter could shoot again in order to obscure her vision. She then retrieved her shield and flipped off of Romanoff's car while blocking more gunfire, until she decided to disarm her of her pistol with a kick. [[File:Black Widow (film) 42.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster analyzing [[Black Widow]]'s moves]] After attempting to throw her shield at Romanoff again, the spy attempted to subdue her with her traditional head-scissor throw, but Taskmaster used the same technique against her, resulting in the both of them lying on the ground. Taskmaster kip-upped into a fighting position nearly identical to Romanoff, much to the latter's bewilderment. [[File:Black Widow (film) 31.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster lunging towards [[Black Widow]]]] As Taskmaster attempted to collect the Red Dust from the crashed car, Romanoff used a grappling hook to tie a wire around her leg, launching Taskmaster into the air. However, Taskmaster then proceeded to use her [[Taskmaster's Sword|sword]] to cut herself free, leaping down behind Romanoff, just as she was attempting to take the Red Dust for herself. Although Romanoff was able to use the shield to defend herself, Taskmaster was able to kick her off the bridge, before discovering that the Red Dust had been taken.{{Ref|BW}} ====[[Chase for the Red Dust]]==== [[File:Taskmaster driving her vehicle.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster chases [[Natasha Romanoff|Romanoff]] and [[Yelena Belova|Belova]]]] Taskmaster eventually managed to track down [[Natasha Romanoff]] and [[Yelena Belova]] in [[Budapest]], where she and her team of [[Black Widows]] ambushed them and began chasing them through the streets. With Romanoff and Belova using a motorbike to drive through the streets, Taskmaster chased them in an armored car, plowing through the other cars in an attempt to catch up. Eventually, Romanoff and Belova got away from Taskmaster, crashing their motorbike in the process. [[File:BW FT 44.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster draws an explosive arrow]] With Romanoff and Belova managing to steal a car, Taskmaster continued to chase them down, as she managed to drive behind them, before getting out of the roof to get a better look. Taskmaster had then armed herself with her [[Taskmaster's Bow|bow and arrows]], as she used her HUD to line up the perfect shot, before shooting an explosive arrow underneath their car, which caused Romanoff and Belova to crash, rolling their car down a flight of stairs and into a [[Nyugati Station|train station]]. [[File:TaskmasterChasesTargetsDownStairs.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster pursues [[Natasha Romanoff|Romanoff]] and [[Yelena Belova|Belova]]]] Taskmaster proceeded to get out of her armored car and followed her targets into the train station, witnessing them pulling themselves out of the crashed car and attempting to flee. As Taskmaster then observed Romanoff and Belova leaping down the stairway, she proceeded to launch her [[Taskmaster's Shield|shield]] after them, which was thrown perfectly down the stairs, and barely missed her targets, becoming embedded in a support beam, while Taskmaster still continued to follow her targets. [[File:TaskmasterChasesTargets-LowAngle.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster loses [[Natasha Romanoff|Romanoff]] and [[Yelena Belova|Belova]]]] Getting to the bottom of the stairs, Taskmaster had then lost sight of her targets, but observed a trail of blood, left behind by the wounded Belova. Taskmaster followed this blood trail, which led her to a manhole cover, which she assumed they had used to get away. Taskmaster then stepped through the manhole, and chased down Romanoff and Belova, only to discover that this had been a deception, and Romanoff and Belova had been able to find another way to escape from Taskmaster.{{Ref|BW}} ====Capturing the Targets==== [[File:BW FT 23.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster arriving to capture her targets]] Taskmaster was contacted by [[Melina Vostokoff]], informing her that [[Natasha Romanoff]] and [[Yelena Belova]] had come to her home to seek information against the [[Red Room]], as well as attempting to learn [[Dreykov]]'s location. Following Vostokoff's lead, Taskmaster led her team of agents into Vostkoff's home, where they found swiftly found and captured [[Red Guardian]], who had been swiftly subdued with multiple tranquilizers being shot directly into his chest. [[File:Taskmaster in Melina's home.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster enters [[Melina Vostokoff]]'s [[Melina Vostokoff's Homestead|home]]]] With Taskmaster arriving onboard her [[Solovyov Air Helicopter|aircraft]], she stepped inside the home, where she discovered that Vostokoff had already managed to subdue both Romanoff and Belova, having taken them off guard and used her [[Black Widow's Bite|Widow's Bite]] to electrocute them both. Once Taskmaster entered, Vostokoff stepped forward, suggesting that they bring Romanoff, Belova and Red Guardian back to the [[Red Room Academy]], where they could be interrogated and then executed by Dreykov.{{Ref|BW}} ====Secrets Revealed==== [[File:Taskmaster in the background.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster stands in [[Dreykov]]'s office]] {{Quote|What? You want to make her feel better? You want to tell her you’re sorry? Well, you should have thought of that before you [[Budapest Operation|blew]] her face off.|[[Dreykov]] to [[Natasha Romanoff]]|Black Widow (film)}} Having successfully captured her targets, Taskmaster had taken them back to the [[Red Room Academy]], where she put [[Red Guardian]] and [[Melina Vostokoff|Natasha Romanoff]] into their cells. Taskmaster had then returned to General [[Dreykov]]'s office, where he was greeted by [[Black Widow|Melina Vostokoff]], as Taskmaster stood by while Dreykov and Vostokoff discussed what would happen to [[Yelena Belova]], who was due to be killed during their experimentations on her mind controlling. [[File:Taskmaster & Dreykov.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster threatening [[Natasha Romanoff]]]] However, during the discussion, Taskmaster had then witnessed Dreykov removing the [[Photostatic Veil]] from Vostokoff, revealing her to actually be Romanoff in disguise, as part of her attempt to assassinate Dreykov. Although Taskmaster responded by immediately drawing her gun, she was ordered to stand down by Dreykov. Taskmaster then stood by, as Dreykov and Romanoff discussed her past, including the death of Romanoff's [[Natasha Romanoff's Mother|mother]], which Dreykov showed no sympathy for as he mocked her. [[File:Antonia Dreykov (Taskmaster).png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster reveals herself to [[Black Widow]]]] In response, Romanoff asked Dreykov if he felt anything when his daughter was seemingly killed when she and [[Clint Barton]] had bombed Dreykov's [[Dreykov's Budapest Office|Office]], unaware of her survival. As the result, Dreykov then turned to Taskmaster, and ordered her to remove her [[Taskmaster Suit|helmet]], revealing her identity and horrifically scarred face to Romanoff, who was shocked at the revelation, while Dreykov explained how they were able to survive the explosion, and how she turned into the [[Red Room]]'s most skilled assassin. [[File:Taskmaster in Black Widow.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster being ordered to kill the traitors]] Seeing Romanoff's reaction to Taskmaster's identity, Dreykov had mocked her by asking if she intended to apologize to her, noting that it would not make her feel better. Dreykov had also admitted that he was disgusted by his daughter's burns, which was why he kept her identity hidden behind her helmet. Knowing that Red Guardian was working with Vostokoff and Belova to sabotage the Academy, Dreykov sent Taskmaster to assassinate them, as Taskmaster left Romanoff and Dreykov alone.{{Ref|BW}} ====Battling Red Guardian==== [[File:Taskmaster claws.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster prepares to fight [[Red Guardian]]]] {{Quote|There's nothing for me to do, and I want to, I want to break something!"
"There's something you can break.|[[Red Guardian]] and [[Melina Vostokoff]]|Black Widow (film)}} Following the orders of [[Dreykov]], Taskmaster went to confront [[Red Guardian]] and [[Melina Vostokoff]], who had managed to get out of their cells, and were discussing the next step of their plan to bring down the [[Red Room Academy]]. While Vostokoff left them alone, Taskmaster faced Red Guardian, who got ready to fight, while Taskmaster drew her claws, based on [[T'Challa|Black Panther]]'s designs, and they began to fight. [[File:Taskmaster beat Red Guardian.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster fighting against [[Red Guardian]]]] During their clash, Taskmaster threw her [[Taskmaster's Shield|shield]] at Red Guardian, who managed to block it, before Taskmaster drew her knife and began slashing at him, using tactics copied from [[Winter Soldier]]. Despite Red Guardian's superior strength, Taskmaster was able use his tactics against him, throwing Red Guardian through a glass wall, before reclaiming her shield and continuing the fight, striking Red Guardian repeatedly, and throwing her shield at his back. [[File:Taskmaster locked up.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster being locked inside of the cell]] With Red Guardian struggling to get back onto his feet, Taskmaster walked up behind him and got him into an arm lock, before drawing a hidden blade from her [[Taskmaster Suit|suit]], which she attempted to stab into the back of Red Guardian's neck. However, before she could deliver the final blow, Taskmaster was unexpectedly tackled by Vostokoff, who threw Taskmaster into one of the cells and locked her inside, leaving Taskmaster furiously punching at the protective glass, angered by the defeat.{{Ref|BW}} ====[[Destruction of the Red Room]]==== [[File:Taskmaster freed by Nat.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster watches [[Natasha Romanoff]]]] {{Quote|I'm not going to leave you here.|[[Natasha Romanoff]] to Taskmaster|Black Widow (film)}} Taskmaster was left inside her cell by [[Melina Vostokoff]], as she witnessed the [[Red Room Academy]] beginning to fall out of the sky and explode. However, Taskmaster was eventually discovered by [[Natasha Romanoff]], who told Taskmaster that she could not allow her to die in the academy, despite knowing that Taskmaster would attempt to kill her. Ignoring the way Taskmaster furiously punched at the protective glass, Romanoff opened the cell door and allowed her to walk free. [[File:Black Widow & Taskmaster.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster is freed by [[Natasha Romanoff]]]] Taskmaster had then stepped out of her cell, and prepared to attack Romanoff, determined to finally get her revenge for the [[Budapest Operation|bombing]] that had scarred her face and destroyed her life. However, before Taskmaster could attack and kill Romanoff, an explosion caused the ceiling between them to collapse, which in turn destroyed the floor underneath Taskmaster, causing her to fall to the level below, while Romanoff was then able to make her escape from the exploding academy.{{Ref|BW}} ====Freedom from Control==== [[File:Taskmaster vs. Black Widow.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster fighting against [[Black Widow]]]] {{Quote|Is [[Dreykov|he]] gone?|Taskmaster to [[Black Widow]]|Black Widow (film)}} With the [[Red Room Academy]] falling from the sky, Taskmaster eventually caught up with [[Natasha Romanoff]], who had just saved [[Yelena Belova]] from falling to her death as they both floated towards the ground with a parachute. Determined to kill Romanoff, Taskmaster leapt off the academy and dove towards her, armed with her [[Taskmaster's Sword|sword]]. Romanoff let go of Belova and prepared to fight against Taskmaster in midair, using some of the falling debris from the academy to attempt to slow her fall. [[File:Taskmaster with sword.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster prepares to fight [[Black Widow]]]] Taskmaster dove directly at Romanoff, causing them to smash through a falling glass wall while Romanoff punched at her and avoided her sword. As the pair grappled in midair, Romanoff was able to pull Taskmaster's parachute just in time, as the pair awkwardly crash landed on the ground. Getting back onto her feet, Taskmaster had then armed herself with her sword and prepared to kill Romanoff, as the pair grappled for control over the sword, with Romanoff managing to take it from Taskmaster. [[File:Black Widow leaked TV spot 7.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster grapples against [[Black Widow]]]] Although Romanoff still refused to kill Taskmaster, dropping the sword on the ground, due to the guilt she felt for almost killing her in [[Budapest]], Taskmaster remained determined and furiously grabbed Romanoff by the throat and attempting to strangle her. However, Romanoff was able to break free from Taskmaster's grip, and managed to get behind her, pressing buttons on Taskmaster's [[Taskmaster Suit|suit]], which caused her helmet to come off, before Romanoff threw Taskmaster down to the ground. [[File:Taskmaster - Red Dust.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster being freed with the [[Red Dust]]]] However, before Taskmaster could strike at Romanoff again, Romanoff broke the final vial of [[Red Dust]], causing the powder to hit Taskmaster in the face, freeing her from her mind control. Taskmaster then froze in mid-punch, staring in shock and amazement at Romanoff, having finally regained control of her mind again. Dropping to the ground, Antonia reached out to Romanoff, questioning if [[Dreykov]] was dead, which Romanoff confirmed, as he had been killed in an earlier explosion. [[File:Taskmaster and Black Widow.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster questioning if [[Dreykov]] is dead]] With Dreykov dead and the [[Red Room]] destroyed, Antonia was unsure what she would do next. They were then rejoined by the surviving members of the [[Black Widows|Widows]], who had also been freed from Dreykov's mind control. Having been given the opportunity to build new lives for themselves, the Widows took Antonia into their care, with [[Lerato]] helping Antonia onto her feet, and bringing her onboard the plane they used to escape before the authorities had arrived.{{Ref|BW}} ===Working for the Contessa=== ====Continued Assassin Work==== Although Taskmaster was free to live her own life, she found herself falling back into what she was best at and was employed by [[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]] to complete assassination missions. Taskmaster was provided with a new suit and proceeded to work for de Fontaine for many years.{{Ref|TB}} ====[[Battle at the O.X.E. Vault|Sent to Die]]==== [[File:Taskmaster (Thunderbolts*).jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster arrives to kill [[U.S. Agent]]]] {{Quote|I'm not here for you.|Taskmaster to [[Yelena Belova]]|Thunderbolts*}} [[2027|Eleven years later]], Taskmaster received a mission from [[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]] to kill [[U.S. Agent]], who would be at an [[O.X.E.]] [[O.X.E. Vault|vault]]. Taskmaster arrived at the vault and found U.S. Agent fighting [[Yelena Belova]], so she saved Belova by kicking U.S. Agent away. Taskmaster looked to Belova and told her that she was not there to kill her. [[File:Taskmaster dead.png|{{AltFloat}}|Taskmaster is killed by [[Ghost]] in the vault]] Taskmaster then continued her mission and attacked U.S. Agent, but he was able to fend her off from killing him. The two clashed, and U.S. Agent knocked Taskmaster away. They then noticed the arrival of [[Ghost]], who phased through U.S. Agent and attacked Taskmaster. The two fought viciously against each other, with Taskmaster knocking her away. While U.S. Agent and Belova fought, Taskmaster went to attack the distracted U.S. Agent. However, Ghost appeared in front of Taskmaster and shot her in the head, killing her. Her body was later looted by U.S. Agent and Ghost and was left in the vault to be incinerated.{{Ref|TB}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== {{Quote|He can mimic anyone he's ever seen. It's like fighting a mirror.|[[Yelena Belova]] to [[Natasha Romanoff]]|Black Widow (film)}} *'''Photographic Reflexes''': [[File:Black Widow (film) 41.jpg|thumb|250px|Taskmaster mimicking [[Black Widow]]]] By viewing how another person fights, Taskmaster was able to perfectly replicate his or her fighting style and movements. She was able to retain this information and use previously replicated fighting styles against different opponents, and could virtually predict her enemies' attacks. Taskmaster had been shown to copy the fighting styles of [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]], [[Black Widow]], [[Hawkeye]], [[T'Challa|Black Panther]], [[Winter Soldier]], and [[Spider-Man]]. While she could replicate a style, she could not copy any innate powers, and she could still be hampered if she does not have access to the same equipment, such as her version of Captain America's [[Captain America's Shield|shield]]. She also did not copy the dominant hands of the individuals, as Hawkeye and Black Widow are left-handed, while she was a right-handed archer. This ability was thanks to the electronic chip implanted in her neck, and was augmented by the HUD in her helmet. *'''Taskmaster Suit Capabilities''': Taskmaster's suit provided several tactical advantages in battle, such as enhanced strength and retractable weaponry. **'''Enhanced Strength''': Taskmaster's suit granted her artificially elevated strength,{{Ref|MSCEUE}} which allowed her to send Black Widow and [[Red Guardian]] back long distances with her strikes. She could also throw her shield with enough force for it to embed itself into metal beams. During the fight in the vault, she managed to knock [[John Walker]] back with a single kick in an attempt to save [[Yelena Belova]] and defeat her target. During the fight against [[Ghost]], she was able to throw her with a single shield strike. **'''Enhanced Durability''': Taskmaster’s suit seems to provide greater resistance to impact trauma, as it allowed her to endure blows from enhanced individuals such as [[John Walker]] and [[Ava Starr]], and get up moments later. ====Replicated Skills==== {| class="wikitable" width="100%" |- ! width="15%" |Individual ! width="15%" |Image ! width="70%" |Description |- | style="text-align:center" |'''[[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]''' | style="text-align:center" |[[File:Black Widow (film) 46.jpg|thumb|250px]] |Taskmaster copied [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]]'s skill with his [[Captain America's Shield|shield]], even owning [[Taskmaster's Shield|one]] herself. She can throw her shield with force and accuracy, and even stomp on it to make it return to her grip similar to what Rogers did after his [[Chase of Captain America|fight]] in an elevator. |- | style="text-align:center" |'''[[Black Widow]]''' | style="text-align:center" |[[File:Black Widow leaked TV spot 4.png|thumb|250px]] |Taskmaster copied [[Black Widow]]'s agile martial arts techniques and gymnastic maneuvers, as she was seen watching footage of the [[Infiltration into Hammer Industries Headquarters]]. |- | style="text-align:center" |'''[[Hawkeye]]''' | style="text-align:center" |[[File:BlackWidowTeaserTrailer58.png|thumb|250px]] |Taskmaster copied [[Hawkeye]]'s skill with a bow and arrow, as she was seen watching him fight [[T'Challa|Black Panther]] during the [[Battle at Leipzig-Halle Airport]]. |- | style="text-align:center" |'''[[T'Challa|Black Panther]]''' | style="text-align:center" |[[File:Taskmaster copies Black Panther.png|thumb|250px]] |Taskmaster copied [[T'Challa|Black Panther]]'s claw-based fighting style, as seen when she fought the [[Red Guardian]]. |- | style="text-align:center" |'''[[Winter Soldier]]''' | style="text-align:center" |[[File:Taskmaster vs. Red Guardian.png|thumb|250px]] |Taskmaster copied the [[Winter Soldier]]'s knife-wielding skills, as seen when she fought the [[Red Guardian]] in a manner similar to the [[Battle of Washington, D.C.|duel]] between the former and [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]] in [[Washington, D.C.]] She even replicated his knife flip. |} ===Abilities=== *'''Master Martial Artist''': [[File:BW FT 36.png|thumb|250px|Taskmaster fighting against [[Red Guardian]]]] Having studied and copied the fighting styles of most of the [[Avengers]] during their [[Battle at Leipzig-Halle Airport|battle]] at the [[Leipzig-Halle Airport|airport]], primarily [[Black Widow]], [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]], and [[T'Challa|Black Panther]], Taskmaster became an extremely skilled and formidable hand-to-hand combatant and martial artist, surpassing all other Widows, making her one of the most dangerous and deadliest fighters on the planet. She was able to fight Black Widow twice, defeating her the first time, though was cured of her brainwashing during the second fight. She even defeated and almost killed the super-soldier [[Red Guardian]]. During her first duel with Black Widow, she effortlessly reversed the latter's head-scissor throw. While mimicking [[T'Challa|Black Panther]], she utilized razor-sharp claws and his swiping techniques against Red Guardian. During the fight in the O.X.E. Vault, Dreykov directly dueled the U.S. Agent on equal terms, facing a highly trained super soldier and former war veteran. However, she was defeated by his superior physical strength, being thrown backwards. Minutes later, she confronted Ghost, a former S.H.I.E.L.D. agent and trained assassin, and on two separate occasions managed to gain the upper hand over Starr. However, she was not fast enough to dodge a shot from her. *'''Shield Mastery''': [[File:Black Widow (film) 34.jpg|thumb|250px|Taskmaster throwing her [[Taskmaster's Shield|shield]] at [[Black Widow]]]] Having studied and copied the fighting style of [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]], Taskmaster is highly skilled at using a [[Taskmaster's Shield|shield]] in combat, and throwing it with deadly precision. She used her shield to deflect gunfire from Black Widow, and she was able to accurately throw it down an escalator to reach her. Against [[Red Guardian]], she used the shield's blunt edges to strike him. Like Captain America, she can stomp on the shield's edge to flip it into her hands. *'''Knife Mastery''': Having studied and copied the fighting style of [[Winter Soldier]], Taskmaster is highly skilled at using a knife in combat. This is seen during the fight against Red Guardian. She also attempted to stab Red Guardian with retractable blade in her gauntlet before being flung into a cell by Melina Vostokoff. *'''Sword Mastery''': Taskmaster is highly skilled at using a [[Taskmaster's Sword|sword]] in combat, best demonstrated during both fights against Black Widow. She was able to cut a trick wire around her leg before it pulled her all the way up. *'''Master Marksman''': [[File:Taskmaster explodes car 2.png|thumb|250px|Taskmaster launching an explosive arrow]] Having studied and copied the marksmanship of [[Hawkeye]], Taskmaster is an extremely skilled marksman, with very accurate aim when using a bow, with additional features like an explosive device. She was able to overturn Romanoff and Belova's vehicle by ricocheting an explosive arrow from the ground to underneath their vehicle. Her skill extends to firearms, as she was later prepared to shoot Romanoff with her revolver until her father ordered her to stand down. *'''Master Acrobat''': Having studied and copied the gymnastic feats of Black Widow, Black Panther, Spider-Man, Captain America, Winter Soldier, and Hawkeye, Taskmaster become an extremely skilled master gymnast, acrobat and aerialist, being able to perform highly acrobatic feats during fights. This can be seen in her fights against Black Widow and Red Guardian. *'''Master Spy''': Taskmaster is highly skilled in espionage, stealth, disguise, infiltration, and demolitions, surpassing all other Widows. {{Quote|You just sent away the one thing that would stop me from killing you.|[[Black Widow]] to [[Dreykov]]|Black Widow (film)}} *'''Master Assassin''': Serving as Dreykov's personal enforcer, Taskmaster is the best assassin in the Red Room, surpassing all other Widows. Therefore, Taskmaster would only be sent on the most important and dangerous missions, often being tasked to track and eliminate the defectors from the Red Room. Years later, [[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]] dispatched her to kill [[U.S. Agent]] and nearly succeeded. *'''Bilingualism''': Taskmaster is fluent in both her native Russian and English. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== *'''[[Taskmaster's Shield|Shield]]''': [[File:Black Widow (film) 33.jpg|thumb|250px|Taskmaster blocking gunfire]] Taskmaster carried a metallic circular shield similar to the one used by [[Captain America]]. The shield is retractable and magnetically attached to Taskmaster's gauntlets. The shield is made of a durable bulletproof material and shaped aerodynamically so that it can be thrown. Upon ambushing [[Natasha Romanoff]] to obtain the [[Red Dust]], Taskmaster used her shield to block Romanoff's gunshots, before attempting to launch the shield at her. Taskmaster would later use the shield during her clash against [[Red Guardian]], only to be defeated and have Red Guardian steal the shield. *'''[[Taskmaster's Sword|Sword]]''': Taskmaster carried a collapsible single edged sword similar in appearance to a katana or ninjato sword. The sword is stored on the back compartment of the [[Taskmaster Suit|suit]]. Upon being tied up with Romanoff's grappling hook, Taskmaster drew her sword to cut herself free, before continuing to battle against Romanoff. Taskmaster would also use her sword, as she attempted to kill Romanoff following the destruction of the [[Red Room Academy]], using it to slow her fall from the crashing base by grinding it through the debris. Upon landing on the ground, Taskmaster continued using the sword against Romanoff, until she was disarmed and had Red Dust thrown in her face, ending her brainwashing. Taskmaster kept the sword with her the following years as she worked for [[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]]. When she was sent to the [[O.X.E. Vault]] to kill [[U.S. Agent]], Taskmaster used the sword to fight her target and [[Ghost]]. However, Ghost got the upper hand and shot Taskmaster dead before looting her corpse and taking her sword. *'''[[Taskmaster's Bow|Bow]]''': [[File:Thunderbolts* Trailer 21.png|thumb|250px|Taskmaster aiming her bow at [[U.S. Agent]]]] Taskmaster carried a blue and orange foldable composite bow similar to the one used by [[Hawkeye]], which was used to fire an explosive arrow to overturn Romanoff and [[Yelena Belova]]'s vehicle. Taskmaster was able to use her helmet's HUD display to ensure that she would have the perfect shot with the bow. **'''Explosive Arrows''': Taskmaster carried explosive arrows like Hawkeye, which was used to overturn Romanoff and Belova's vehicle, managing to get the perfect shot under their car, causing it to crash. *'''Chiappa Rhino''': Taskmaster had this revolver holstered on her right thigh. Taskmaster drew this gun on [[Black Widow]] to protect [[Dreykov]], only for Dreykov to order her to put it away, due to his knowledge that Black Widow would not actually be able to kill him. *'''Combat Knife''': Taskmaster carried a tanto style combat knife on her which was used during her fight against [[Red Guardian]]. ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Taskmaster Suit|Suit]]''': Taskmaster wore a hooded tactical armored suit when operating on missions. The helmet is retractable and can be removed by a button on the right gauntlet. **'''HUD''': Taskmaster's mask has a heads-up display which analyzes battlefield data. Using this HUD, Taskmaster was able to predict her opponents' movement patterns, which she used against [[Natasha Romanoff]] during their first confrontation, allowing her to able to overpower Romanoff with ease. Taskmaster would later use the HUD to assist with her aim while using her [[Taskmaster's Bow|bow]] to shoot at Romanoff and [[Yelena Belova]]'s car, finding the perfect moment to shoot underneath the car and cause it to crash. **'''Retractable Claws''': [[File:Black Widow Promo Photos from 06.23 (4).jpeg|thumb|250px|Taskmaster unleashes her retractable claws]] Taskmaster's gloves contain retractable metal claws similar to those of [[T'Challa|Black Panther]], which were used against [[Red Guardian]]. **'''Retractable Gauntlet Blade''': Taskmaster's left gauntlet contains a retractable blade similar to the ones used by Crossbones which was used in an attempt to kill Red Guardian. However, [[Melina Vostokoff]] managed to attack and entrap Taskmaster before she could kill Red Guardian with the blade. ===Vehicles=== *'''APC''': Taskmaster rode an APC while [[Chase for the Red Dust|pursuing]] [[Natasha Romanoff]] and [[Yelena Belova]] through the streets of [[Budapest]]. ==Facilities== *'''[[Dreykov's Budapest Office]]''': In her youth, Antonia lived in [[Budapest]] and would visit her [[Dreykov|father]] in his office after school. As part of her defection to [[S.H.I.E.L.D.]], [[Natasha Romanoff]] collaborated with [[Clint Barton]] to [[Budapest Operation|attempt to assassinate]] Dreykov, for which she used Antonia to lead her to him before destroying the building with explosives. Despite having seemingly died in the explosion, Antonia and her father both survived, with Antonia sustaining heavy injuries. *'''[[Red Room Academy]]''': After being disfigured in the attempt on her father's life, Antonia, with a chip inserted into her neck, was made to go through his academy and was uniquely trained under the [[Taskmaster Protocol]], gaining the power to copy people's fighting styles. She eventually graduated to become the leader of the [[Black Widows]]. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *[[Dreykov]] † - Father and Former Enthraller ===Allies=== *[[Black Widows]] - Former Colleagues **[[Lerato]] - Caretaker **[[Ingrid (Widow)|Ingrid]] † **[[Helen (Widow)|Helen]] **[[Finnish Widow]] *[[Black Widow|Natasha Romanoff/Black Widow]] † - Former Attempted Killer and Former Attempted Victim *[[Melina Vostokoff]] - Former Enemy *[[Yelena Belova]] - Former Enemy *[[Red Guardian|Alexei Shostakov/Red Guardian]] - Former Enemy {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[S.H.I.E.L.D.]] **[[Hawkeye|Clint Barton/Hawkeye]] - Attempted Killer *[[Red Room]] - Former Allies *[[Oksana]] † - Former Colleague *[[O.X.E.]] **[[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]] - Recruiter *[[U.S. Agent|John Walker/U.S. Agent]] - Target *[[Ghost|Ava Starr/Ghost]] - Killer {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== In chronological order: *{{Appearance|BW}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|TA}} (mentioned) *{{Appearance|BW}} *{{Appearance|TBD}} (files) *{{Appearance|TB}} ==Trivia== *In the comics, '''Anthony "Tony" Masters''', better known as '''Taskmaster''', was a mercenary hired as a training instructor by various criminal organizations such as [[Hydra]] and [[A.I.M.]], who had the uncanny ability to mimic the fighting moves of his opponents. **Some of the people he has copied include (but are not limited to) [[Ant-Man]], [[Black Knight]], [[T'Challa|Black Panther]], [[Black Widow]], [[Bullseye]], [[Steve Rogers|Captain America]], [[Captain Marvel]], [[Daredevil]], [[Elektra]], [[Sam Wilson|Falcon]], [[Hawkeye]], [[Iron Fist]], [[Iron Man]], [[Moon Knight]], [[Punisher]], [[Shang-Chi]], [[Spider-Man]], and [[U.S. Agent]]. He also trained U.S. Agent, [[Crossbones]], and [[Red Skull]]. However, he cannot copy [[Deadpool]] due to his unpredictable nature. *In a deleted scene from ''Black Widow'', Taskmaster is seen being arrested by the [[United States Army]], under the supervision of [[Thaddeus Ross]]. *Dreykov is 5'10.[[:File:Taskmaster Trading Card Back.jpg|Taskmaster Trading Card]] ==Behind the Scenes== *[[Andy Lister]] was a stunt double and the in-suit performer for Olga Kurylenko in the role of Taskmaster. [[Daren Nop]], [[Amy Sturdivant]], [[Eniko Fulop]] and [[Agel Aurélia]] were also stunt doubles for Kurylenko in the role. *An early draft for ''[[Black Widow (film)|Black Widow]]'' did feature Taskmaster as Tony Masters like in the comic books, but his inclusion was scrapped as the film was meant to be a spy thriller with [[Dreykov]] as the main antagonist while dealing with the mystery of who Dreykov's daughter was, leading [[Eric Pearson]] to merge Taskmaster and Dreykov's daughter into the same character.[https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/black-widow-writer-interview-taskmaster-ending-eric-pearson/ Black Widow Writer Eric Pearson on Budapest, Taskmaster, and Post-Credits Scene (Exclusive)] *An early draft of ''[[Thunderbolts*]]'' had Taskmaster be present throughout the film. She was to bond with [[Ava Starr]] over their similar histories and there would have been a running gag where she frequently forgot that she and [[John Walker]] were no longer enemies. Eric Pearson revealed that he was surprised by her removal, but added that it was most likely done due to the memory lapse subplot being closer to [[Sentry|Bob]]'s story.[https://www.polygon.com/marvel/596372/thunderbolts-taskmaster-death-planned-story-arc Thunderbolts*’ writer didn’t know Marvel cut his Taskmaster arc until he saw the movie] ==References== {{Gallery|Taskmaster/Gallery}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{WP|Taskmaster (comics)|Taskmaster}} *{{Marvel.com|characters/taskmaster}} [[es:Antonia Dreykov]] [[fr:Antonia Dreykov]] [[pl:Taskmster]] [[ru:Таскмастер]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Black Widow (film) Characters]] [[Category:Thunderbolts* Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Cyborgs]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Brown Hair]] [[Category:Green Eyes]] [[Category:Assassins]] [[Category:Red Room Trainees]] [[Category:Red Room Leaders]] [[Category:O.X.E. Operatives]] [[Category:Enhanced Individuals]] [[Category:Multilingual Characters]] [[Category:Brainwashed Characters]] [[Category:Deceased Characters]] [[Category:Characters Killed by Ghost]] [[Category:Genderbent for the Marvel Cinematic Universe]]
